ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for sambal cauliflower as a prepared dish. Closest defensible anchor is the cauliflower food product class; sambal preparation context is not captured.
ontology.foodex2_description: Cauliflower — used as closest FoodEx2 anchor for this stir-fried cauliflower dish with sambal belacan. No composite dish code exists for sambal cauliflower in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for sambal cauliflower as a prepared composite dish. A00LC (cauliflower) is used as the primary ingredient anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical side dish serving of sambal cauliflower is approximately 150 g, equivalent to a medium bowl or side plate portion as served in a Singaporean or Malaysian hawker meal.

nutrient_source.title: Sambal Cauliflower — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database (HPB)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sambal Cauliflower (cr_id F14080024). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated at 0.6 g/100g based on typical cooking oil and belacan composition consistent with HPB methodology for similar stir-fried vegetable dishes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sambal cauliflower as a prepared dish. GI estimated at 15 based on cauliflower as the dominant ingredient (GI ~15, non-starchy cruciferous vegetable, University of Sydney GI Database). The sambal belacan and chilli components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the GI. Glycemic load per 150 g serving = (15 × 1.86 g carbohydrate per 100g × 1.5) / 100 = 0.42, rounded to 0.4. This is a very low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 347 mg per 100 g from belacan (fermented shrimp paste)
hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 347 mg/100 g is moderate and primarily derived from belacan; hypertensive individuals should limit portion size to 100 g or request reduced belacan preparation, and avoid adding further high-sodium condiments such as fish sauce or soy sauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate 1.86 g/100 g, low GI ~15, and high dietary fibre 3.35 g/100 g
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sambal cauliflower is well-suited for Type 2 Diabetes management due to its very low carbohydrate content, estimated GI of 15, and high fibre which slows glucose absorption; a 150 g serving contributes only approximately 2.8 g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of 0.4.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat ~0.6 g/100 g, high dietary fibre 3.35 g/100 g, and antioxidant-rich cruciferous vegetable base
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Sambal cauliflower is broadly cardioprotective due to its low saturated fat, high fibre content supporting LDL reduction, and sulforaphane-rich cauliflower base; sodium at 347 mg/100 g should be monitored in individuals with heart failure or sodium-restricted CVD management plans.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade ingredients (chilli) and borderline sodium 347 mg/100 g approaching 400 mg RA threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Cauliflower provides anti-inflammatory sulforaphane and fibre supporting gut microbiome immune regulation; however, chilli in sambal is a nightshade and may aggravate RA symptoms in sensitive individuals, and sodium should be minimised by requesting reduced belacan.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sambal cauliflower is a popular Singaporean and Malaysian Malay stir-fried vegetable dish consisting of cauliflower florets cooked in sambal belacan — a pungent, spicy paste made from dried chilli, shallots, garlic, and belacan (fermented shrimp paste block). The dish is typically prepared by stir-frying blanched or raw cauliflower in a wok with the sambal paste until the florets are tender-crisp and well-coated with the aromatic, umami-rich sauce. The flavour profile is bold, spicy, and savoury with a characteristic fermented seafood depth from the belacan. Nutritionally, sambal cauliflower is a low-calorie, low-carbohydrate, high-fibre side dish providing 52 kcal, 2.2 g protein, 4.0 g fat, 1.86 g carbohydrate, and 3.35 g dietary fibre per 100 g. Sodium is moderate at 347 mg/100 g due to the belacan content. The dish is suitable for Type 2 Diabetes management given its very low GI (~15) and negligible glycemic load. It is broadly cardioprotective and suitable for weight management. Individuals with hypertension or rheumatoid arthritis should moderate portion size due to sodium content and nightshade sensitivity respectively. Cauliflower is a cruciferous vegetable rich in sulforaphane and vitamin C, contributing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The dish is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free but is not vegetarian or vegan due to belacan (shrimp paste).

notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for sambal cauliflower as a prepared dish are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F14080024 or available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific composite dish as of 2026. Saturated fat estimated at 0.6 g/100g based on typical cooking oil and belacan composition for similar HPB stir-fried vegetable dishes. GI of 15 is an analogous estimate based on cauliflower (dominant ingredient) from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for sambal cauliflower as a prepared dish. The input record tagged this dish as vegetarian and plant_based in dietary_tags; this has been corrected to is_vegetarian: false and is_vegan: false because belacan is a fermented shrimp (crustacean) product. Keto tag retained as the dish is very low in carbohydrates (1.86 g/100g). Crustacean allergen flagged due to belacan content.
